citation.description: test_code_review_emit_retains_the_cap asserts exactly RETAIN_PER_TICKET events remain after N>50 emits for code-review. test_prune_bound_still_respected_after_prose_fields (tests/interfaces/store/test_sidecar_reader.py:139-160) covers the plan-review path.

criteria.criterion: After 50+ reviews on one ticket, up to 50 REVIEW_RESULT sidecars are retained and a write past 50 prunes the oldest — for BOTH plan-review and code-review. Oracle: unit tests appending N>50 synthetic events on each path and asserting retained count is exactly 50.

citation.description: test_retention_cap_is_50_and_a_single_shared_definition asserts plan_sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET == 50, code_sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET == 50, they are equal, and both prune defaults are 50. plan_review/sidecar.py:48 defines it; code_review/sidecar.py:22 imports it.

criteria.criterion: The retention cap is a SINGLE definition: plan_review.sidecar defines RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 50 and code_review.sidecar obtains the SAME value by importing it. Oracle: test asserting both == 50 and each prune defaults keep to that constant.

citation.description: emit() calls prune(target_ticket, repo_root=repo_root) after append_event. test_code_review_emit_calls_prune (test_audit_retention_cap.py:95-101) verifies the wiring via monkeypatch.

criteria.criterion: code_review.sidecar.emit prunes after appending (previously-absent path now exists). Oracle: a test that emitting more than the cap on a code-review target ticket leaves exactly 50 events.

citation.description: All tests are real (no skip/xfail), implementation is complete and non-aspirational. Cannot execute make commands from static inspection, but there are no structural issues observable in the code or tests.

criteria.criterion: make lint && make typecheck && make test pass
